name: ZEBRA TECHNOLOGIES CORP
business: Zebra Technologies designs, manufactures, and sells a broad range of automatic identification and data capture (AIDC) offerings including mobile computers, barcode scanners and imagers, RFID readers, specialty printers, machine vision, self-serve touchscreen solutions, related accessories and supplies, and cloud-based software subscriptions. The company sells to end-users in retail and e-commerce, manufacturing, transportation and logistics, healthcare, hospitality, public sector, and other industries globally through a direct sales force and a network of over 10,000 channel partners.

exposures.sentence: We also provide machine vision and self-serve touchscreen solutions; a full range of services, including maintenance, technical support, repair, managed and professional services; as well as cloud-based software subscriptions.

Our supplies business also includes electronic sensors and temperature-monitoring labels which incorporate chemical indicators designed to change color upon exceeding predefined time and/or temperature thresholds, used in vaccine distribution and other use cases in healthcare and food safety.
Our fixed industrial scanning products automatically track and trace items that move from production through distribution.

Industrial applications include inventory management in warehouses and distribution centers; field mobility applications include field service, post and parcel, and direct store delivery; and retail and customer facing applications include e-commerce, omnichannel, mobile point of sale, inventory look-up, staff collaboration, and analytics.

Additionally, artificial intelligence is further streamlining frontline workflows by providing insights and delivering actions using on-device vision, audio, location and RFID to automate parts of traditional workflows.

We also expect to drive growth by capitalizing on technology transitions occurring in the industry, including transitions to WiFi 7, the 5th generation mobile network (5G) and Android operating system in mobile computing and transitions in data capture to technologies involving 2D and 3D imaging and RFID, among others.
End-users of our offerings include those in retail and e-commerce, manufacturing, transportation and logistics, healthcare, hospitality, public sector, and other industries.
End-users of our offerings include those in retail and e-commerce, manufacturing, transportation and logistics, healthcare, hospitality, public sector, and other industries.
End-users of our offerings include those in retail and e-commerce, manufacturing, transportation and logistics, healthcare, hospitality, public sector, and other industries.
In the fourth quarter of 2025, we announced our intention to dispose of or exit our robotics automation solutions business in an effort to better align resources with our strategic priorities.
Cybersecurity incidents could disrupt our business operations.

The need to transform workflows is being driven by secular trends in technology, which include the creation of digital twins, cloud-based data analytics, automation, mobility, computer vision, artificial intelligence and machine learning.
Data from enterprise assets, including status, condition, location, utilization, and preferences, is analyzed in the cloud to provide prioritized actionable insights and optimize activities.

We also provide machine vision and self-serve touchscreen solutions; a full range of services, including maintenance, technical support, repair, managed and professional services; as well as cloud-based software subscriptions.
Emerging issues related to the development and use of artificial intelligence (AI) could give rise to legal or regulatory action, damage our reputation or otherwise materially harm our business.

catalysts.event: Announced intention to exit robotics automation solutions business, incurring one-time costs
catalysts.sentence: In the fourth quarter of 2025, we announced our intention to dispose of or exit our robotics automation solutions business in an effort to better align resources with our strategic priorities.

catalysts.event: Committed to 2025 Productivity Plan with estimated costs of $35-40 million and annualized pre-tax operating cost savings of at least $20 million, substantially completed in 2026
catalysts.sentence: In the fourth quarter of 2025, the Company committed to certain organizational changes designed to generate cost efficiencies while better aligning our organizational structure with the Company's long-term growth strategy (collectively referred to as the 2025 Productivity Plan).

catalysts.event: Expected capital expenditures of $85 million to $95 million in 2026
catalysts.sentence: We also expect to spend approximately $85 million to $95 million on capital expenditures in 2026.

catalysts.event: Committed to repurchase $500 million of shares over the following twelve months from October 2025
catalysts.sentence: In October 2025, we announced our commitment to repurchase $500 million of shares over the following twelve months, of which $403 million had already been repurchased as of February 5, 2026.
